calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar May-19-2025 Continued To Jul-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gary Rothstein appeared via zoom for (petitioner).
Fatima Evans appeared via zoom for University of California, Berkeley.

Discussion was held regarding the possibility of expanding the pool of applicants for the scholarship, that how this will be administered and if it's possible to be administered by the staff that is working there now, and that there is approximately $3,000,000 for the scholarship fund and approximately 4 to 5% annually return on the monies. The matter is continued to July 21, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Rene Auguste Chouteau. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Thompson. Not reported.

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement (Petitions)
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gary D. Rothstein for US Bank, Petitioner.
Fatima Evans for Regents of the University of California, for UC Berkeley.
Thomas O'Keefe for Carolita "Kit" Chick Phillips, Agent for Torodel Partnership.

The court stated that it had reviewed the submitted papers, noting that the petition was well-framed and that the settlement appeared fair. The court called for any objections. No objections were presented.

The court took the Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Continued To Oct-28-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Nov-21-2018 Continued To Dec-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Gary Rothstein for MUFG Union Bank.

Mr. Rothstein stated that everybody (the bank and the beneficiaries) seems on board with this petition. The Court noted Professor Chick's statement "'if absolutely necessary" is not proof she is fully on board, . Mr. Rothstein stated he is happy to try to get something from Professor Chick, that she has been unresponsive, and requested 30 days. The Petition for order modifying trust for changed circumstances is continued to December 19, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow Mr. Rothstein opportunity to obtain a clearer statement from Professor Chick this is what she wants and that it is not being forced on her.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Continued To Oct-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for MUFG Union Bank, N.A. (Petitioner)

Mr. Rothstein spoke of his second supplement, stating there are no objections, Professor Chick (the sole beneficiary of the Trust) has agreed with modifying investments, and Union Bank can submit proposals to her. The Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Trustee is Taken Under Submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Continued To May-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gary Rothstein for Union Bank.

Mr. Rothstein addressed the issue of notice to UC Berkeley and Torodel stating that Professor Chick has the absolute right to withdraw all of the assets, and that when a party has that right and she is the only Beneficiary, notice needs to be given only to the Beneficiary. Mr. Rothstein also stated that it is not unreasonable for the Trustee to take action when it is in the best interest of the Beneficiaries, that the cash was getting very little return and no growth, spoke of the requirement of diversification of the assets and State regulations, the issue of Ms. Chick's silence, and stated that he has left messages with the school, that the school staff has made no indication that she is no longer there and that he thinks she is still alive. The Petition for Order Settling First Account and Report of Trustee is continued to May 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: B. Wan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Mar-12-2018 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Oct-28-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for (petitioner/trustee) Union Bank.

The Court stated that it has the supplement to the petition filed 10/13/16, asked if there is anything further and that the trust provides that the beneficiary has the authority to make decisions. Mr. Rothstein stated that that is why they submitted the second supplement, that it is to strike a balance between the beneficiary and the trustee, that the beneficiary would retain the ability to advise, that it just pushes her to make decisions, that they have not been able to reach her in two years with the exception of two email responses saying that she is busy and that they have tried to reach her through the school but only get voicemail. The Court stated that it is surprised that there is no response. Mr. Rothstein stated that that is the trustee's issue as well. The Court stated that it has a problem, that article 7 states that there is to be no purchase, sale or exchange without the okay from Roselind. Mr. Rothstein stated that they do not think the controller will abide by that and that there are concerns regarding the requirements of diversification. The Court stated that it does not know that they have the right to do this. Mr. Rothstein stated that the trustor intended that her daughter would take an active role. The Court stated that the trustor could have provided for the successor trustee not acting, that it is hard for the court to go against the trust language and that there is no liability to the trustee to follow the language. Mr. Rothstein stated that no objections have been made by the beneficiary. The Court stated that it wonders if she is receiving the messages. Mr. Rothstein stated that they do not know for sure but cannot believe that she is not, that they assume that she receives the distributions and that they have not heard to the contrary. The Court took the Petition for Order Modifying Trust for Changed Circumstances under submission.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for (petitioner/trustee) Union Bank.

The Court asked if counsel had anything to add. Mr. Rothstein stated that he read the examiner's notes, that the investment power granted by the trustor implies the trustors intent, that the trustor thought the assets as being in her own trust, that the power was passed to Dr. Chick and that Dr. Chick has not been affirmatively acting towards the investments. The Court stated that that is not grounds for the petition. Mr. Rothstein stated that the trustor could not anticipate that Dr. Chick would do nothing regarding the investments, that there are regulatory requirements, that the relief that they are seeking is different than the prior petition, that they structured the proposal so that Dr. Chick retains power regarding the investments and can veto the request within 5 days of notice from the bank, that notice will consist of description of the change and will be sent to Dr. Chick within 24 hours via email or overnight delivery, that Dr. Chick is in London, that they can make the veto time longer if necessary, confirmed that there are remainder beneficiaries who will take if Dr. Chick does not withdraw all of the funds. The Court stated that it is not inclined to grant the petition but would grant a continuance to get authorization from Dr. Chick. Mr. Rothstein stated that they have tried, that Dr. Chick does not object but does not respond either and that it puts the bank in a difficult situation. The Court stated that it can either continue for consent or will take the matter under submission. Mr. Rothstein requested a continuance. The Petition for Order Modifying Trust for Changed Circumstances was continued to October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Modifying Trust For Changed Circumstances
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Rothstein for petitioner MUFG Union Bank.

The Court inquired whether Mr. Rothstein had anything he wished to argue on the matter. Mr. Rothstein responded in the negative, stating that he belived the petition was complete and no objection had been made so he was willing to submit the matter to the Court for decision. He added that the issue was whether the bank would be permitted to meet its obligations to the contingent as well as current beneficiaries by placing trust funds in investments with a higher rate of return that cash savings, such as the stock market. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: John Boncato. (Not reported)
